tactical_shape: Julian Nagelsmann’s side were described as procedural and dominant, with threats emanating from all around the pitch
They created a stream of opportunities after the drinks break and finished ruthlessly against a front-foot Curaçao
standout_players: Kai Havertz — efficient, rounded things off with his second goal and converted the penalty nonchalantly
Felix Nmecha — finished beautifully for the opener and won the penalty
Jamal Musiala — chopped in a confidence-boosting angled finish
Nathaniel Brown — impressive left-back, crossed for Schlotterbeck’s goal and steered in a deft volley
Deniz Undav — tapped in off the bench
Joshua Kimmich — supplied the slide-rule pass for Musiala
Florian Wirtz — provided the smart return pass for the opener
Nico Schlotterbeck — glanced in from a corner
home_read.momentum: Germany look comfortably on course for the knockouts, though the piece stresses tougher tests are still to come.

tactical_shape: Dick Advocaat set them up to give it a go and they tried to play more offensively despite the risks
They worked spaces with clever footwork and continued to attempt a front-foot approach even after falling behind
standout_players: Livano Comenencia — equalised against Germany and scored a historic goal
Tahith Chong — used clever footwork to help Curaçao warm into the game
Jürgen Locadia — seized on the loose ball before Comenencia’s equaliser
Eloy Room — busy, denied Schlotterbeck earlier
Leandro Bacuna — had a late chance to score
Jearl Margaritha — also had a late chance to score
away_read.momentum: A heavy defeat, but the goal and the support generated a memorable, emotional moment that the article treats as a proud chapter for Curaçao.
